Event Timeline: Discovery of the Screw (S3, Matrix 3)
10:24 PM: While emptying the trays inside the machine, the operator noticed a screw. The operator immediately notified the Team Lead, Supervisor, QC, and Maintenance personnel.
10:36 PM: The production line was halted.
10:38 PM: Maintenance began investigating the source of the screw and reported that other parts were missing. A search was initiated, but the missing parts were not found. The missing parts were a nut and a washer.
10:40 PM: The last pallet to exit Matrix 3 was sent through the X-ray scanner; no foreign objects were detected.
10:50 PM: An X-ray test was conducted on Matrix 3 using similar parts; these parts were successfully rejected by the system.
10:55 PM: Maintenance reinstalled the removed part and replaced the missing component.
11:00 PM: All pallets produced by Matrix 3 during the preceding hour were placed on hold. An update regarding the exact quantity of affected pallets will be provided shortly.
11:09 PM: A total of 4 pallets and 1 partial pallet were identified as affected.

Can you briefly describe the reasons for high scrap Grey?

We stopped the line for 16 minutes, we were having issues on Cavanna, the product came out open.

The main reasons for the high scrap today were startup issues and multiple machine adjustments while trying to get the line running correctly. We had Cavanna adjustments because the product was coming out open, and we also experienced several line stops while Maintenance and technicians worked on different machine issues. In addition, when the line stops for extended periods, the chocolate in the hopper loses temper and needs to be removed, which also increased scrap. We are also trying to stop Imperial every time Cavanna stops to help minimize scrap. Unfortunately, scrap is still high right now, but as the line continues to run better and adjustments are completed, we expect the scrap percentage to improve.
The Belt C6, Imperial, and metal detector are running correctly.
